---
species: "Mastocarpus stellatus"
accepted_code: "MAST"
alternate_codes: []
diminutive: ""
alternate_diminutives:
common_name: "False irish moss"
field_id_possible:
  - yes
description: "Fan-like branching red algae with channeled fronds. Central/main branch tends to be thicker and triangle-like."

image: '/header_images/mastocarpus_stellatus.jpg'
categories:
  - erect red algae
sampling_programs:
    - keen_quad
    - bhi_quadrat
    - bhi_rov
    - bhi_bruv
habitat:
  - intertidal
  - subtidal
mobility:
  - sessile
sampling_region:
  - GOM
  - Virginian
catami_code: 80300903
catami_version: "1.4"
worms_code: 145650
---

![](/header_images/mastocarpus_stellatus.jpg)

Photo from Ignacio Bárbara via [WORMS](https://www.marinespecies.org/aphia.php?p=image&pic=14893&tid=145650)

**Other images:** 
<br/>

**Distinguishing features:** Has channeled fronds. Does NOT have a blue iridescence underwater and in some lighting. When reproductive, can be covered in small cysts/bumps, resembling grape pips.
<!-- size, gross morphology, a key identifier, zonation notes (intertidal or depth) -->
<br/>

**Easily confused species and differentiation:** Chondrus Crispus (CHCR)
*Chondrus* has a flat stipe/thalli, although the edges of the blades might be curly. *Mastocarpus* stipes/thalli are curled. See [here](https://mainenaturalhistory.org/chondrus-crispus-vs-mastocarpus-stellatus/) for the definitive guide.
<br/>

**Extended Description:** Red algae with fan-like dichotomous branching. Fronds are thick and leathery. It is often twisted. Can be found intertidally or in the shallow subtidal. 
<!-- Extended description -->

**[WORMS page](https://www.marinespecies.org/aphia.php?p=taxdetails&id=145650)**
